questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following entities has NOT been a presenter or sponsor of the Booker Prize since its inception in 1969?
questions.explanation: The Booker Prize has been presented by Booker, McConnell Ltd (1969–2001), Man Group (2002–2019), and Crankstart (2019 onwards). The Commonwealth Foundation is not mentioned as a presenter.
questions.takeaway: The sponsorship of the Booker Prize has evolved through three distinct phases: Booker McConnell, Man Group, and currently Crankstart.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Presented by: Booker, McConnell Ltd (1969–2001); Man Group (2002–2019); Crankstart (2019 onwards).

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The work 'Heart Lamp', which recently won the International Booker Prize, was originally written in which of the following languages?
questions.explanation: Banu Mushtaq won the International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp', which is a Kannada short story collection.
questions.takeaway: Banu Mushtaq's 'Heart Lamp' is a landmark win for Kannada literature on the international stage.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Banu Mushtaq wins International Booker Prize for Kannada short story collection 'Heart Lamp'.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Since 2019, which of the following organizations has been the presenter of the Booker Prize?
questions.explanation: The Booker Prize was presented by Booker, McConnell Ltd from 1969 to 2001, followed by the Man Group from 2002 to 2019. From 2019 onwards, it has been presented by Crankstart.
questions.takeaway: The sponsorship of the Booker Prize has transitioned through three main entities since its inception in 1969.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Presented by: Booker, McConnell Ltd (1969–2001); Man Group (2002–2019); Crankstart (2019 onwards)

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the eligibility criteria for the Booker Prize, which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: It is restricted to authors who are citizens of Commonwealth nations.
It is open to English-language novels from any country, provided they are published in Britain and Ireland.
The work must be a non-fiction historical account or a biography.
The prize is awarded biennially to authors for their entire body of work.

questions.explanation: The Booker Prize is open to English-language novels from any country that have been published in Britain and Ireland. It is an annual prize for fiction, not restricted to the Commonwealth.
questions.takeaway: The Booker Prize has evolved from a Commonwealth-centric award to one open to English-language fiction globally, provided it meets the UK/Ireland publication criteria.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Founded in 1969, the Booker Prize is open to English-language novels from any country that have been published in Britain and Ireland.
